1.original: Here are suggestions to help you develop creative power through belief.

3.original: Eliminate the word impossible from your thinking and speaking vocabularies.

5.original: The thought it's impossible sets off a chain reaction of other thoughts to prove you're right.

7.original: Think of something you've been wanting to do but felt you couldn't.

8.original: Now make a list of reasons why you can do it.

9.original: Many of us whip and defeat our desires simply because we concentrate on why we can't,

10.original: when the only thing worthy of our mental concentration is why we can.

11.original: Recently I read a newspaper item that said there are too many counties in most states.

12.original: The article pointed out that most county boundaries were established decades before the first automobile was built,

13.original: and while the horse and buggy was the chief mode of travel.

14.original: But today, with fast automobiles and good roads, there is no reason why three or four counties could not be combined.

15.original: This would cut down greatly on duplicated services so that taxpayers would actually get better service for less money.

16.original: The writer of this article said he thought he had stumbled across a really live idea,

17.original: so he interviewed 30 people at random to get their reactions.

19.original: Not one person thought the idea had merit, even though it would provide them with better local government at less cost.

20.original: That's an example of traditional thinking.

21.original: The traditional thinker's mind is paralyzed.

22.original: He reasons, it's been this way for a hundred years, therefore it must be good and must stay this way.

24.original: Average people have always resented progress.

25.original: Many voiced a protest toward the automobile on the grounds that nature meant for us to walk or use horses.

26.original: The airplane seemed drastic to many.

27.original: Man had no right to enter the province reserved for birds.

28.original: A lot of status-quoers still insist that man has no business in space.

29.original: One top missile expert recently gave an answer to this kind of thinking.

30.original: Man belongs, says Dr. Von Braun, where man wants to go.

31.original: Around 1900, a sales executive discovered a scientific principle of sales management.

32.original: It received a lot of publicity and even found its way into textbooks.

34.original: There is one best way to sell a product.

35.original: Find the best way, then never deviate from it.

36.original: Fortunately for this man's company, new leadership came in in time to save the organization from financial ruin.

37.original: Contrast that experience with the philosophy of Crawford H. Greenwalt, president of one of the nation's largest business organizations,

39.original: In a talk at Columbia University, Mr. Greenwalt said, There are many ways in which a good job can be done,

40.original: as many ways, in fact, as there are men to whom the task is given.

41.original: In truth, there is no one best way to do anything.

42.original: There is no one best way to decorate an apartment, landscape a lawn,

43.original: make a sail, rear a child, or cook a steak.

44.original: There are as many best ways as there are creative minds.

46.original: If we let tradition freeze our minds, new ideas can't sprout.
